preamble: The spirantization of the voiceless uvular plosive /q/ to the voiceless uvular fricative /χ/ is documented in several Quechua dialects of South America, where the Proto-Quechua uvular stop has undergone lenition. The process preserves the uvular place of articulation while weakening the manner from a complete closure to a fricative constriction.

examples.note: Second-person singular pronoun; /q/ is realized as voiceless uvular fricative [χ] especially in positions following voiceless sibilants, reflecting ongoing spirantization of the uvular stop.
Guinea pig; another instance of the q→[χ] spirantization in intervocalic contexts.
In Junín Quechua (Quechua I branch, Peru), Proto-Quechua *q has undergone a completed spirantization to a voiceless fricative, realized as [χ] or [x] depending on phonological environment.

sources: Gallagher, G. (2023). The phonetic realization of the plain uvular /q/ in a variety of South Bolivian Quechua. Journal of the International Phonetic Association, 53(3), 869-887.
Adelaar, W. F. H., & Muysken, P. (2004). The Languages of the Andes. Cambridge University Press.
